claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, could you please tell me how many children you're looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to know you have Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now, could you please tell me whether you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that.

Next, could you tell me about your living arrangements with Alex? Does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Wonderful, thank you. That's helpful to know.

Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One final question: Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any type of residential accommodation, or has Alex been in any of these situations recently?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, actually, I'm fostering Alex. But I don't get any money from the council for looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know that important detail about fostering Alex.

Just to confirm: you mentioned you don't receive any money from the council. Could you clarify whether the local council is paying anything at all towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance (such as housing costs, food, clothing, or other living expenses)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. I'm covering all those costs myself.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. Based on the information you've provided, I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, I'm pleased to tell you that **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

- **Age requirement**: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16 ✓
- **UK residence**: You live in the UK ✓
- **Responsibility**: Alex lives with you, so you are responsible for them ✓
- **Fostering**: Although you are fostering Alex, the local council is not paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance, which means you still qualify ✓
- **No other claimant**: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex ✓

You can proceed to make a claim for Child Benefit. Is there anything else you'd like to know?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, meaning they are responsible for the child. Although Alex is being fostered, the local council is not paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. According to the rules, you can get Child Benefit if you foster a child, as long as the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is fostering one child (Alex, aged 8) and lives in the UK. They live with Alex and are covering all costs themselves, with no financial contribution from the local council towards accommodation or maintenance. As the fostering rules state that Child Benefit can be claimed when the council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance, and all other basic eligibility criteria are met, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and Alex lives with them, meaning they are responsible for the child. Although Alex is being fostered, the local council is not paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. According to the rules, you can get Child Benefit if you foster a child, as long as the local council is not paying anything towards their accommodation or maintenance. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is fostering one child (Alex, aged 8) and lives in the UK. They live with Alex and are covering all costs themselves, with no financial contribution from the local council towards accommodation or maintenance. As the fostering rules state that Child Benefit can be claimed when the council is not paying towards accommodation or maintenance, and all other basic eligibility criteria are met, the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for Alex.
